title: Proclamation No. 735, s. 1970
text:
PROCLAMATION NO. 735

DESIGNATING THE PERIOD, FROM JULY 1 TO SEPTEMBER 30, 1970 FOR THE SECOND ANNUAL EDUCATIONAL AND FUND CAMPAIGN OF THE COMMUNITY CHEST OF CEBU, INC.

To enable the Community Chest of Cebu, Inc., to carry on its community welfare services, I, ALEJANDRO MELCHOR, Executive Secretary, by order of His Excellency, FERDINAND E. MARCOS, President of the Philippines, do hereby designate the period from July 1 to September 30, 1970; for its second annual educational and fund campaign.

I call upon all residents, firms and organizations especially in the City of Cebu, Lapulapu City, Toledo City, Mandawe City, Danao City and the municipalities of Northern and Southern Cebu, to support this campaign and to give generously to the Community Chest of Cebu, Inc., which unites into one annual appeal the 20 fund campaigns of the following Welfare agencies:

1. Cebu Good Shephered Convent 2. Cebu Girls’ Center, Inc. Cebu Boys’ Center, Inc. 3. (a) Cebu Boys Town 4. (b) Pasil Youth Center 5. Cebu Society for Crippled Children 6. Cebu Children’s Community Kitchen Franciscan Missionaries of Mary 7. (a) St. Anthony’s Charity Clinic 9. (c) Our Lady of Lourdes Dispensary 8. (b) St. Joseph’s Charity Clinic 10. (d) St. Isidoro Charity Clinic 11. Family Life Workshop of the Philippines (Cebu Chapter) 12. Cebu YMCA 13. Family Life Workshop of the Philippines (Lapulapu City) 14. Friends of the Lepers, Inc. 15. St. Martin de Porres School for Retarded Children 16. St. Theresa’s Medical-Social Center 17. Cebu YWCA 18. Young- Christian Workers (Girls) of Cebu 19. Young Ladies Association of Charity 20. Society of St. Vincent de Paul

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Republic of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done in the City of Manila, this 7th day of August, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and seventy.

(Sgd.) ALEJANDRO MELCHOR Executive Secretary

By the Executive Secretary:

(Sgd.) ROBERTO V. REYES Acting Assistant Executive Secretary

Source: National Library
